title: Rotarod Test to assess motor coordination in a mouse parkinsonian model
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8t9s3","text":"Protocol for the evaluation of motor coordination in the rotarod assay in parkinsonian mice.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"2lk6e","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5qq1r","text":"(76-0770) Rota Rod with Touchscreen, 5 Mice Harvard Apparatus","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"emtc2","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"33f1l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4lloj","text":"Consumables","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"92ic5","text":"- 70% ethanol (ETOH) in water solution: used to sanitize the instrument between subjects","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5d6ar","text":"- Paper towels","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bakn4","text":"- Non-toxic markers: Sharpie® brand (red, blue, black, purple preferred)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Training Phase: 3 days Testing Phase: 3 days
Four trials separated by at least 30 min inter-trial intervals at constant velocity.
Four trials separated by at least 30 min inter-trial intervals in accelerating mode from 4 to 40 rpm in 3000s.

Latency of fall. The cumulative time (seconds) the subject maintains its balance on the rotarod prior to falling off of the rod. The maximum latency is set at 300 seconds per trial. The analysis should cover the latency of fall (when applies): - by day - by phase - by group: control vs parkinsonian - by treatment: control vs l-dopa

Prior to placing the subjects onto the rod, confirm the trip plates are in the active position, the rod is rotating at the start speed of 0 RPM and the reset button has been clicked. Place the mice onto the rotating rod in sequential order of rod lanes from lane 1 on the far left of the rod to lane 5. Immediately upon all mice being placed onto the rod, press the START button on the instrument panel to start the timers. As the mice fall from the rod, each lane’s trip plate will stop the timer and display the latency in minutes and seconds. Once all mice in a trial have fallen and placed into their cages remove all gross urine and feces with a dry paper towel. The instrument must be thoroughly sanitized with 70% ethanol.

Prior to placing the subjects onto the rod, confirm the trip plates are in the active position, the rod is rotating at the start speed of 4 RPM and the reset button has been clicked. Place the mice onto the rotating rod in sequential order of rod lanes from lane 1 on the far left of the rod to lane 5. Immediately upon all mice being placed onto the rod, press the START button on the instrument panel to start the timers. As the mice fall from the rod, each lane’s trip plate will stop the timer and display the latency in minutes and seconds. Once all mice in a trial have fallen and placed into their cages remove all gross urine and feces with a dry paper towel. The instrument must be thoroughly sanitized with 70% ethanol.
